title: The Complexity of Modern Business
article_text: The complexity of modern business is a continuous challenge. Surprisingly, many companies fail to extract useful data from their operations. A bias in decision-making can become a serious liability. The implementation of new guidelines should have been done carefully. It is the golden rule that we must consume resources wisely. We catalog every weekly report to track our progress. A sharp knife can bite into the envelope of our assumptions. Wherever we go, we must stamp our unique mark on the market. Biological tissues are barely used in tourism, but they are important in medicine. The luxury of a simple narrative was invented to reduce complexity.

sentences.en: The complexity of modern business is a continuous challenge.
sentences.zh: 现代商业的复杂性是一个持续的挑战。

words.eng_def: the state of being difficult to understand or deal with
happening without stopping

sentences.en: Surprisingly, many companies fail to extract useful data from their operations.
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，许多公司未能从他们的运营中提取有用的数据。

words.eng_def: in a way that causes surprise
to take out something from something else

sentences.zh: 决策中的偏见可能成为一个严重的负担。

sentences.en: The implementation of new guidelines should have been done carefully.
sentences.zh: 新指南的实施本应谨慎进行。

sentences.en: It is the golden rule that we must consume resources wisely.
sentences.zh: 黄金法则是我们必须明智地消耗资源。

words.eng_def: made of gold; very good or important
to use something such as time, energy, or food

sentences.en: We catalog every weekly report to track our progress.
sentences.zh: 我们将每份周报编目以跟踪我们的进展。

words.eng_def: to make a list of things
happening or done once a week

sentences.en: A sharp knife can bite into the envelope of our assumptions.
sentences.zh: 一把锋利的刀可以切入我们假设的信封。

sentences.en: Wherever we go, we must stamp our unique mark on the market.
sentences.zh: 无论我们去哪里，我们都必须在这个市场上留下我们独特的印记。

sentences.en: Biological tissues are barely used in tourism, but they are important in medicine.
sentences.zh: 生物组织在旅游业中很少使用，但在医学中很重要。

vocabulary.eng_def: the state of being difficult to understand or deal with
vocabulary.example: The complexity of the problem surprised everyone.

vocabulary.eng_def: something that causes problems or makes you responsible
vocabulary.example: His old car became a liability.

vocabulary.eng_def: in a way that causes surprise
vocabulary.example: Surprisingly, the test was easy.

vocabulary.eng_def: to take out something from something else
vocabulary.example: We extract oil from the ground.

vocabulary.eng_def: the process of making something start to happen
vocabulary.example: The implementation of the new law took time.

vocabulary.eng_def: a strong feeling for or against something without good reason
vocabulary.example: The judge showed no bias.

vocabulary.eng_def: in a way that is not the same
vocabulary.example: We see the world differently.

vocabulary.eng_def: to make a list of things
vocabulary.example: They catalog all the books in the library.

vocabulary.eng_def: happening without stopping
vocabulary.example: There was continuous rain all day.

vocabulary.eng_def: made of gold; very good or important
vocabulary.example: She has a golden opportunity.

vocabulary.eng_def: to put a mark on something using a special tool
vocabulary.example: Please stamp the date on the paper.

vocabulary.eng_def: a rule or instruction about how to do something
vocabulary.example: Follow the safety guidelines.

vocabulary.eng_def: a flat paper container used to send letters
vocabulary.example: I put the letter in an envelope.

vocabulary.eng_def: a tool used for cutting
vocabulary.example: He cut the bread with a knife.

vocabulary.eng_def: related to living things
vocabulary.example: Biological science studies life.

vocabulary.eng_def: to use something such as time, energy, or food
vocabulary.example: We consume a lot of water.

vocabulary.eng_def: something expensive that is not necessary but gives pleasure
vocabulary.example: A car is a luxury for some people.

vocabulary.eng_def: happening or done once a week
vocabulary.example: We have a weekly meeting.

vocabulary.eng_def: in or to any place
vocabulary.example: Wherever you go, I will follow.

vocabulary.eng_def: to cut into something with teeth or a sharp tool
vocabulary.example: The dog will bite if you scare it.

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“令人惊讶地”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'in a way that causes surprise'?
Which word means 'the process of making something start to happen'?
Which word means 'to use something such as time, energy, or food'?

quiz.question_cn: 哪个词的意思是“每周的”？
quiz.question_en: Which word means 'happening or done once a week'?
Which word means 'a strong feeling for or against something without good reason'?

image_prompts: A complex web of business connections and charts showing continuous challenge.
A surprised person looking at a computer screen with data extraction icons.
A person weighing scales with a bias symbol on one side and a heavy liability on the other.
A team carefully following a large guideline document during implementation.
A golden rule book with a person wisely consuming resources like food and water.
A librarian cataloging a stack of weekly reports on a desk.
A sharp knife cutting into an envelope that has question marks coming out of it.
A person stamping a unique mark on a world map with arrows pointing everywhere.
A biological tissue sample in a lab, with a tourism poster barely visible in the background.
A luxurious simple storybook with a complex puzzle dissolving into it.
